lines.translation: Historiquement, la Chine était une société qui privilégiait les hommes par rapport aux femmes, où le statut des femmes dans la famille et la société était bien inférieur à celui des hommes.
Après 1950, la situation a commencé à changer progressivement.
Surtout dans les villes, les filles et les garçons avaient les mêmes opportunités en matière d'éducation et d'emploi, et le statut social des femmes s'est considérablement amélioré.
Depuis la Réforme et l'Ouverture, le phénomène d'inégalité des sexes est réapparu dans certaines entreprises et institutions publiques.

Par exemple, il est souvent plus difficile pour les femmes de trouver un emploi que pour les hommes ; certaines usines et entreprises n'ont pas encore appliqué le principe à travail égal, salaire égal.
Bien sûr, il y a aussi des femmes qui surpassent l'homme moyen, tant dans leur travail que dans leurs revenus.
Cependant, elles ne représentent qu'une minorité parmi les femmes.

Mais dans de nombreuses familles chinoises, maris et femmes prennent soin l'un de l'autre, de sorte que la famille est peut-être l'endroit le plus égalitaire pour les hommes et les femmes dans la société chinoise actuelle.
Prenons l'exemple de l'oncle de Lin Xuemei ; c'est un grand fan de football et ce n'est que lorsqu'il regarde un match à la télévision qu'il ne fait aucune tâche ménagère.
Mais dès que le match est terminé, il s'empresse d'aider sa femme à cuisiner et à faire la vaisselle, redevenant ainsi un 'mari modèle'.
Oncle, comment se fait-il que tu fasses la vaisselle aujourd'hui ?
Haha, parce que je fais la vaisselle plus proprement que ta tante.
Laisse-moi les faire ! Je fais aussi la vaisselle très proprement.

Non, non, non, tu reviens tout juste de ton voyage au Yunnan et tu es déjà occupée à chercher du travail et à passer des entretiens, tu ne t'es pas encore reposée. En plus, ton oncle a regardé le match jusqu'à une heure du matin hier soir, ne devrait-il pas faire bonne figure aujourd'hui ?
Oncle, quel match y avait-il hier soir ?
Un match de football masculin, l'équipe de Pékin contre l'équipe de Shanghai.

Qu'est-ce qu'il y a de si bien là-dedans ? Je ne supporte pas ces joueurs de football masculins. Ils sont généralement incroyablement arrogants, mais ils perdent toujours leurs matchs. Alors, je ne regarde que l'équipe féminine chinoise ; elles sont bien meilleures que l'équipe masculine.

C'est vrai, l'équipe féminine a de meilleurs résultats que l'équipe masculine, mais j'ai entendu dire que les joueuses gagnent seulement un dixième de ce que gagnent les joueurs masculins. C'est vraiment injuste.
N'oublie pas, leurs revenus sont déterminés par le marché. Les salaires des joueuses de basket professionnelles aux États-Unis sont aussi bien inférieurs à ceux des joueurs masculins.
Hé, ne parle pas toujours de la défaite de l'équipe masculine chinoise. Je viens de voir une nouvelle en ligne disant que l'équipe masculine chinoise a battu l'équipe championne du monde hier.
Je n'y crois pas. Ce doit être n'importe quoi trouvé sur internet, n'est-ce pas ?

Il semble que ce soit vrai. Internet dit que l'équipe masculine chinoise a bien joué cette fois-ci, le score était de 0-0 pendant longtemps. À la dernière minute, l'équipe masculine a marqué un but et a gagné le match 1-0.
Je ne m'attendais pas à ça, vraiment pas, c'est génial ! Alors, cette équipe championne du monde, c'était l'équipe allemande ou l'équipe espagnole ?
L'équipe féminine chinoise de tennis de table.
Hein ?
Alors quelqu'un doit être en train de faire une blague sur l'équipe masculine de football !

language_notes.label: a
language_notes.explanation_en: [zh:逐渐] et [zh:渐渐] signifient tous deux « progressivement », mais [zh:渐渐] est plus courant dans le langage familier.

language_notes.label: b
language_notes.focus_text: 企业单位 vs. 事业单位
language_notes.explanation_en: [zh:单位] signifie « unité de travail », mais dans l'usage contemporain, cela se rapproche de « entreprise ». Les [zh:企业单位] sont des entreprises engagées dans des activités génératrices de profits comme la fabrication, la construction et le commerce. Les usines, les mines et les chemins de fer sont des [zh:企业单位] typiques. Les [zh:事业单位], quant à elles, sont des unités financées par des fonds gouvernementaux, comme les écoles et les hôpitaux.

language_notes.label: c
language_notes.explanation_en: [zh:十分之一] (littéralement « une des dix parties ») signifie « un dixième ».

grammar_sections.title: Phrases avec [zh:是…的] (II)
grammar_sections.explanation_en: En plus de son utilisation pour discuter des détails d'une action passée, la construction [zh:是…的] peut également servir à persuader, rassurer ou influencer quelqu'un. Lorsqu'elle est employée ainsi, l'expression entre [zh:是] et [zh:的] peut être un adjectif ou une locution adjectivale, souvent avec [zh:很] (par exemple, [zh:是很不错的]). Des verbes peuvent aussi être utilisés, mais ils doivent généralement être accompagnés d'un verbe modal (par exemple, [zh:会]), d'un complément de potentiel (par exemple, [zh:找得到]), ou être un verbe mental pouvant être modifié par [zh:很] (par exemple, [zh:重视]). Les verbes seuls ne sont généralement pas employés dans cette construction.

examples.hanzi: 我洗碗也是洗得很干净的。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐ wǎn yě shì xǐ de hěn gānjìng de.
examples.translation: Je suis aussi doué pour faire la vaisselle.

examples.hanzi: 别担心, 医生说你父亲的身体健康情况是很不错的。

examples.hanzi: 我相信住在校内是很安全的。

examples.hanzi: 只知道减肥, 不注意饮食与睡眠, 也不锻炼, 对你是没有好处的。

examples.translation: Se concentrer uniquement sur la perte de poids en ignorant l'alimentation, le sommeil et sans faire d'exercice ne vous fera aucun bien. Ici, [zh:有好处] fonctionne comme un adjectif. (Plus neutre : [zh:只知道减肥, 不注意饮食与睡眠, 也不锻炼, 对你没有好处。])

usage_notes: Utilisez 是…的 pour ajouter une nuance persuasive, rassurante ou emphatique à une affirmation.
Les adjectifs ou les expressions adjectivales peuvent être placés entre 是 et 的. Ils incluent souvent 很.
Les verbes peuvent être utilisés entre 是 et 的 s'ils : a) suivent un verbe modal, comme 会, b) incluent un complément de potentiel, comme -得到, ou c) sont des verbes mentaux modifiés par 很, comme 重视.
Généralement, les verbes seuls ne peuvent pas être insérés dans cette construction.

common_errors.explanation_en: Quand un verbe est utilisé dans cette structure pour indiquer une tendance ou une possibilité, il doit être précédé d'un verbe modal, comme 会.

common_errors.wrong: 小张这个人是不忘这忘那的。
common_errors.correct: 小张这个人是不会忘这忘那的。
common_errors.explanation_en: Quand un verbe est utilisé dans cette structure pour indiquer une tendance ou une possibilité, il doit être précédé d'un verbe modal, comme 会.
Quand un verbe est utilisé dans cette structure pour indiquer un potentiel, il a besoin d'un complément de potentiel, comme 找得到.

grammar_sections.title: 过来 comme complément
grammar_sections.explanation_en: Comme complément, 过来 peut exprimer un sens résultatif : un retour à un état normal et actif.

patterns.meaning_en: Indique un retour à un état normal ou actif après une action.

examples.hanzi: 你昨天太累了，现在休息过来了吗？
examples.pinyin: Nǐ zuótiān tài lèi le, xiànzài xiūxi guolai le ma?
examples.translation: Tu étais si fatigué hier. As-tu récupéré ?

examples.hanzi: 他病得很厉害，医生费了好大力气才把他救过来。
examples.pinyin: Tā bìng de hěn lìhai, yīshēng fèi le hǎo dà lìqi cái bǎ tā jiù guolai.
examples.translation: Il était gravement malade. Le médecin a à peine réussi à le sauver.

examples.hanzi: 他不清楚这件事他错在哪里，老师给他讲了很长时间，他才明白过来。
examples.pinyin: Tā bù qīngchu zhè jiàn shì tā cuò zài nǎlǐ, lǎoshī gěi tā jiǎng le hěn cháng shíjiān, tā cái míngbai guolai.
examples.translation: Il ne voyait pas où il s'était trompé. Le professeur lui a donné une très longue explication, et ce n'est qu'après cela qu'il a compris.

usage_notes: L'opposé de 过来 est 过去. Voici quelques exemples de 过去 comme complément : 死过去了, qui signifie décéder, 睡过去了, qui signifie s'endormir, et 晕过去了 [zh:yūn guo qu le], qui signifie perdre connaissance.

translation: Historically, China was a society that privileged men over women, where women's status in the family and society was much lower than men's.

scenes.scene_goal: 通过视觉对比，展现中国古代重男轻女的社会现象。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 一组蒙太奇镜头，色调为深褐色。一个古代家庭中，男孩被众人围绕庆祝，而女孩则在角落里默默做事。随后镜头切换到一个妇女小心翼翼地给她的丈夫端茶，低着头，不敢直视。
scenes.setting_en: An ancient Chinese family courtyard, Qing dynasty.
scenes.video_prompt_en: A montage of scenes in sepia tone, depicting historical China. A young boy is being celebrated by his family, while a girl of the same age works quietly in the background. A woman in traditional Qing dynasty attire serves tea to her husband, her head bowed deferentially. Cinematic, historical drama style.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 在历史上，中国是一个重男轻女的社会，妇女的家庭地位和社会地位都比男人低得多。
voice_over.pinyin: zài lìshǐ shàng zhōngguó shì yí ge zhòng nán qīng nǚ de shèhuì fùnǚ de jiātíng dìwèi hé shèhuì dìwèi dōu bǐ nánrén dī de duō
voice_over.translation: Historically, China was a society that privileged men over women, where women's status in the family and society was much lower than men's.

hanzi: 一九五○年以后，情况逐渐发生了变化。
特别是在城市里，女孩子和男孩子一样有受教育和参加工作的机会，妇女的社会地位也有了很大的提高。
pinyin: yī jiǔ wǔ líng nián yǐhòu qíngkuàng zhújiàn fāshēng le biànhuà
tèbié shì zài chéngshì lǐ nǚháizi hé nánháizi yíyàng yǒu shòu jiàoyù hé cānjiā gōngzuò de jīhuì fùnǚ de shèhuì dìwèi yě yǒu le hěn dà de tígāo
translation: After 1950, the situation gradually began to change.
Especially in the cities, girls and boys had the same opportunities for education and employment, and women's social status improved greatly.

scenes.scene_goal: 展示1950年代后女性地位的提升，特别是在教育和工作方面。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 画面从深褐色过渡到褪色的彩色，模拟老电影质感。一个1950年代的教室里，男孩和女孩并排坐着，一起举手回答问题。接着，镜头切换到一家纺织厂，一群穿着蓝色工装的女工在生产线上自信地工作，脸上带着笑容。
scenes.setting_en: A 1950s Chinese city classroom and a textile factory.
scenes.camera_movement: Smooth dolly shot across the classroom, then a static wide shot of the factory floor.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Transition from sepia to a faded 1950s film color palette. A classroom in 1950s China, boys and girls sit together, eagerly raising their hands. Cut to a bustling textile factory, female workers in blue uniforms operate machinery with confidence and smiles. Propaganda poster style, optimistic mood.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 一九五○年以后，情况逐渐发生了变化。特别是在城市里，女孩子和男孩子一样有受教育和参加工作的机会，妇女的社会地位也有了很大的提高。
voice_over.pinyin: yī jiǔ wǔ líng nián yǐhòu qíngkuàng zhújiàn fāshēng le biànhuà. tèbié shì zài chéngshì lǐ nǚháizi hé nánháizi yíyàng yǒu shòu jiàoyù hé cānjiā gōngzuò de jīhuì fùnǚ de shèhuì dìwèi yě yǒu le hěn dà de tígāo
voice_over.translation: After 1950, the situation gradually began to change. Especially in the cities, girls and boys had the same opportunities for education and employment, and women's social status improved greatly.

hanzi: 改革开放以来，在一些企业和事业单位，又出现了男女不平等的现象。
比方说，找工作的时候，妇女常常比男人更难；一些工厂和公司还没有做到男女同工同酬。
pinyin: gǎigé kāifàng yǐlái zài yìxiē qǐyè hé shìyè dānwèi yòu chūxiàn le nánnǚ bù píngděng de xiànxiàng
bǐfāng shuō zhǎo gōngzuò de shíhou fùnǚ chángcháng bǐ nánrén gèng nán yìxiē gōngchǎng hé gōngsī hái méiyǒu zuòdào nánnǚ tóng gōng tóng chóu
translation: Since the Reform and Opening-Up, the phenomenon of gender inequality has reappeared in some enterprises and public institutions.
For example, it is often more difficult for women to find jobs than men; some factories and companies have not yet achieved equal pay for equal work.

scenes.scene_goal: 揭示改革开放后重新出现的男女不平等问题，尤其是在求职和薪酬方面。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 画面变为现代、清晰但色调偏冷的风格。一个现代办公室里，一位女求职者和一位男求职者先后接受面试。女求职者简历优秀，但面试官表情犹豫；男求职者简历平平，面试官却热情握手。分屏画面：左边是女员工，右边是男员工，做着同样的工作，但右边男员工拿到的工资单明显更厚。
scenes.camera_movement: Cross-cutting between two interviews, then a static split-screen shot.
scenes.shot_type: Over-the-shoulder shot, close-up on resumes and paychecks.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Clean, cold, modern cinematic style. A highly qualified female candidate is interviewed, the interviewer is hesitant. A less qualified male candidate is interviewed next and is warmly welcomed. Split screen: a man and a woman doing identical office work, at the end of the day, the man receives a visibly thicker pay envelope. Corporate realism.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 改革开放以来，在一些企业和事业单位，又出现了男女不平等的现象。比方说，找工作的时候，妇女常常比男人更难；一些工厂和公司还没有做到男女同工同酬。
voice_over.pinyin: gǎigé kāifàng yǐlái zài yìxiē qǐyè hé shìyè dānwèi yòu chūxiàn le nánnǚ bù píngděng de xiànxiàng. bǐfāng shuō zhǎo gōngzuò de shíhou fùnǚ chángcháng bǐ nánrén gèng nán yìxiē gōngchǎng hé gōngsī hái méiyǒu zuòdào nánnǚ tóng gōng tóng chóu
voice_over.translation: Since the Reform and Opening-Up, the phenomenon of gender inequality has reappeared in some enterprises and public institutions. For example, it is often more difficult for women to find jobs than men; some factories and companies have not yet achieved equal pay for equal work.

hanzi: 当然，也有一些女性，无论在工作上还是收入上，都超过一般男人。
不过她们在女性中只是少数。
pinyin: dāngrán yě yǒu yìxiē nǚxìng wúlùn zài gōngzuò shàng háishì shōurù shàng dōu chāoguò yìbān nánrén
búguò tāmen zài nǚxìng zhōng zhǐshì shǎoshù
translation: Of course, there are also some women who surpass the average man, both in their work and in their income.
However, they are only a minority among women.

scenes.scene_goal: 展示成功的女性是存在的，但强调她们仍是少数，引出普遍现象与个例的区别。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头聚焦于一位气场强大的女高管，她正在顶层办公室的落地窗前打电话，俯瞰着繁华的城市。她的表情自信而从容。然后镜头缓缓拉远，穿过玻璃墙，显示出她只是巨大办公楼里无数个窗口中的一个。最后，镜头快速切换，展现更多普通岗位上的女性，如服务员、清洁工、普通文员，与女高管形成对比。
scenes.setting_en: A luxurious top-floor office in a skyscraper, then various everyday work environments.
scenes.camera_movement: Slow pull-back, zoom out, followed by a quick montage.
scenes.shot_type: Medium close-up, transitioning to extreme wide shot.
scenes.video_prompt_en: A powerful female CEO in a stunning penthouse office overlooking a city skyline, speaking confidently on the phone. The camera slowly pulls back, revealing her as a single figure in a massive skyscraper. Quick cuts to a montage of other women in more common jobs: a waitress, a cleaner, a factory worker, highlighting that the CEO is a minority. High-contrast, cinematic.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 当然，也有一些女性，无论在工作上还是收入上，都超过一般男人。不过她们在女性中只是少数。
voice_over.pinyin: dāngrán yě yǒu yìxiē nǚxìng wúlùn zài gōngzuò shàng háishì shōurù shàng dōu chāoguò yìbān nánrén. búguò tāmen zài nǚxìng zhōng zhǐshì shǎoshù
voice_over.translation: Of course, there are also some women who surpass the average man, both in their work and in their income. However, they are only a minority among women.

hanzi: 哎，别老说中国男足输球了。我刚看到网上的消息说，中国男足昨天赢了世界冠军队。
我不相信，是网上乱说的吧？
pinyin: āi, bié lǎo shuō zhōngguó nánzú shū qiú le. wǒ gāng kàndào wǎngshàng de xiāoxi shuō, zhōngguó nánzú zuótiān yíng le shìjiè guànjūn duì.
wǒ bù xiāngxìn, shì wǎngshàng luàn shuō de ba?
translation: Hey, don't always talk about the Chinese men's team losing. I just saw news online saying the Chinese men's team beat the world champion team yesterday.
I don't believe it. It must be some nonsense from the internet, right?

scenes.scene_goal: 舅舅试图用一个“好消息”来反驳雪梅对男足的批评，雪梅表示怀疑。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 舅舅从沙发上坐直，举着手机，表情夸张地宣布一个好消息。镜头切换到雪梅，她皱着眉，一脸不信地看着舅舅。
scenes.setting_en: Cozy living room of a modern Chinese apartment. Afternoon light streams in.
scenes.camera_movement: Start on Uncle, then quick cut to Xuemei's reaction.
scenes.video_prompt_en: A middle-aged Chinese man (Uncle) sitting on a sofa in a modern living room, holds up his smartphone with a triumphant and slightly mischievous expression. Cut to a young woman (Lin Xuemei) sitting across from him, looking skeptical and dismissive. The lighting is warm and natural.

hanzi: 好像是真的。网上说，这次中国男足踢得不错，比分一直是0比0。最后一分钟，中国男足才踢进一球，以1比0赢了比赛。
pinyin: hǎoxiàng shì zhēnde. wǎngshàng shuō, zhècì zhōngguó nánzú tī de búcuò, bǐfēn yìzhí shì líng bǐ líng. zuìhòu yì fēnzhōng, zhōngguó nánzú cái tījìn yì qiú, yǐ yī bǐ líng yíng le bǐsài.
translation: It seems to be true. The internet says the Chinese men's team played well this time, the score was 0-0 for a long time. In the last minute, the men's team scored a goal and won the game 1-0.

scenes.scene_goal: 舅舅绘声绘色地描述比赛的“细节”，增加可信度。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 舅舅煞有介事地看着手机屏幕，像是在念新闻一样，详细描述了比赛过程。他一边说，一边用手比划着踢球和进球的动作，表情非常投入。
scenes.setting_en: Cozy living room of a modern Chinese apartment.
scenes.camera_movement: Zoom in slowly on Uncle's face as he tells the story.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Close-up shot of the Uncle's face, he is looking at his phone screen as if reading news, narrating a soccer match with great enthusiasm. He makes small gestures with his hands to mimic kicking a ball and scoring a goal. His expression is very convincing and serious.

voice_over.speaker: 舅舅
voice_over.hanzi: 好像是真的。网上说，这次中国男足踢得不错，比分一直是0比0。最后一分钟，中国男足才踢进一球，以1比0赢了比赛。
voice_over.pinyin: hǎoxiàng shì zhēnde. wǎngshàng shuō, zhècì zhōngguó nánzú tī de búcuò, bǐfēn yìzhí shì líng bǐ líng. zuìhòu yì fēnzhōng, zhōngguó nánzú cái tījìn yì qiú, yǐ yī bǐ líng yíng le bǐsài.
voice_over.translation: It seems to be true. The internet says the Chinese men's team played well this time, the score was 0-0 for a long time. In the last minute, the men's team scored a goal and won the game 1-0.

hanzi: 没想到，真的没想到，太棒了！那这个世界冠军队是德国队还是西班牙队？
pinyin: méi xiǎngdào, zhēnde méi xiǎngdào, tài bàng le! nà zhège shìjiè guànjūn duì shì déguó duì háishì xībānyá duì?
translation: I didn't expect that, really didn't expect that, that's great! So was that world champion team the German team or the Spanish team?
The Chinese women's table tennis team.

scenes.scene_goal: 雪梅信以为真，兴奋地追问，舅舅揭晓了笑话的包袱。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 雪梅的表情从怀疑转为惊喜，她身体前倾，兴奋地追问对手是谁。镜头给到舅舅，他放下手机，慢悠悠地、一脸平静地公布了答案。
scenes.setting_en: Cozy living room of a modern Chinese apartment.
scenes.camera_movement: Start with a medium shot of Xuemei's excited reaction, then a quick cut to a deadpan close-up of the Uncle as he delivers the punchline.
scenes.video_prompt_en: The young woman, Lin Xuemei, leans forward, her face lit up with surprise and excitement. She asks a question eagerly. Quick cut to a close-up of the Uncle's deadpan face. He calmly delivers the punchline without smiling, looking directly at her.

hanzi: 那一定是什么人在开男足的玩笑！
pinyin: nà yí dìng shì shén me rén zài kāi nán zú de wán xiào!
translation: Then someone must be making a joke about the men's soccer team!

scenes.scene_goal: 雪梅和舅妈对这个笑话的反应。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 雪梅愣住了，嘴巴微张，一脸错愕。舅妈从厨房探出头来，手里还拿着锅铲，笑着摇了摇头，对这个玩笑做出了总结。舅舅则得意地笑了起来。
scenes.setting_en: Living room, with a view into the adjacent kitchen.
scenes.camera_movement: Start with a close-up on Xuemei's stunned face. Pan slightly to show the Aunt leaning out from the kitchen doorway. End on a wide shot of the three of them, with the Uncle laughing.
scenes.shot_type: Close-up, then medium shot, then wide shot
scenes.video_prompt_en: Extreme close-up on Lin Xuemei's face, her mouth slightly open in stunned silence. The camera pans to the side to reveal the Aunt, wearing an apron and holding a spatula, leaning out from the kitchen doorway, smiling and shaking her head. The shot widens to show all three characters in the living room; the Uncle is now laughing heartily, pleased with his joke.

global_config.teaching_style: Clear, structured, and encouraging, breaking down complex sentences and cultural concepts for intermediate learners.
global_config.visual_style: Clean, modern animated cards with text highlights and simple iconography.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the historical background of gender inequality in China and learn the key idiom '重男轻女'.

narration.subtitle_en: The text begins by setting the historical context. It says, [zh:在历史上], meaning 'in history' or 'historically'. Then it states that China was a society that privileged men over women. The key phrase here is [zh:重男轻女]. Let's break it down: [zh:重] means 'to value' or 'to see as important,' [zh:男] is 'male.' [zh:轻] means 'to belittle' or 'to see as unimportant,' and [zh:女] is 'female.' Together, [zh:重男轻女] describes a society or mindset that values men more than women.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 在历史上，中国是一个重男轻女的社会，
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zài lìshǐ shàng, Zhōngguó shì yí ge zhòng nán qīng nǚ de shèhuì,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Historically, China was a society that privileged men over women,

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to past events or the study of them.
An idiom describing the traditional preference for males over females.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Literally 'heavy male, light female,' vividly capturing the idea of valuing one gender over the other.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to a community of people living in a particular country or region and having shared customs, laws, and organizations.

segments.visual_notes: Show an old-fashioned scroll or black-and-white photo background. Animate the characters of '重男轻女' appearing one by one with their English meanings.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express comparison using '比...得多' and understand the concept of '地位'.

narration.subtitle_en: This part of the sentence describes the consequences of that societal view. It talks about the status of [zh:妇女], a formal term for 'women'. Their [zh:家庭地位], 'family status,' and [zh:社会地位], 'social status,' were both much lower than men's. The word [zh:地位] means 'status' or 'position.' The structure [zh:比男人低得多] is a key comparison. [zh:比] means 'compared to,' [zh:低] means 'low,' and [zh:得多] adds emphasis, meaning 'much more.' So, 'much lower than men'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 妇女的家庭地位和社会地位都比男人低得多。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: fùnǚ de jiātíng dìwèi hé shèhuì dìwèi dōu bǐ nánrén dī de duō.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: women's status in the family and society was much lower than men's.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A formal term for women, often used in social or political contexts.
Refers to a person's social or professional standing.
Used here to describe status, but can also mean low in height or level.

segments.visual_notes: Use a scale icon, with the 'woman' side significantly lower than the 'man' side. Highlight '家庭地位' and '社会地位' as they are mentioned.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the sentence describing a change in the situation and learn the adverb '逐渐'.

narration.subtitle_en: The next sentence marks a turning point. It starts with [zh:一九五○年以后], 'after 1950.' The subject is [zh:情况], which means 'the situation' or 'circumstances.' The key verb phrase is [zh:发生了变化], 'underwent a change.' Notice the adverb [zh:逐渐], which means 'gradually.' It indicates that the change didn't happen overnight. As the language notes mention, [zh:逐渐] is similar to [zh:渐渐], but is slightly more formal.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 一九五○年以后，情况逐渐发生了变化。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yījiǔwǔlíng nián yǐhòu, qíngkuàng zhújiàn fāshēng le biànhuà.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: After 1950, the situation gradually began to change.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to the state of affairs at a particular time.
An adverb indicating a slow, step-by-step process. More formal than '渐渐'.
Can be a noun or a verb, meaning 'change' or 'to change'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a timeline starting from 1950, with a slowly rising graph line to represent 'gradual change'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the specific improvements for women in cities, focusing on education and work opportunities.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence specifies where and how the change happened. It starts with [zh:特别是在城市里], 'especially in the cities.' It then says that girls, [zh:女孩子], and boys, [zh:男孩子], had the same opportunities. The structure [zh:一样有...的机会] means 'equally have the opportunity to...'. What were these opportunities? First, [zh:受教育], 'to receive an education,' and second, [zh:参加工作], 'to participate in the workforce' or 'to get a job'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 特别是在城市里，女孩子和男孩子一样有受教育和参加工作的机会，
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tèbié shì zài chéngshì lǐ, nǚháizi hé nánháizi yíyàng yǒu shòu jiàoyù hé cānjiā gōngzuò de jīhuì,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Especially in the cities, girls and boys had the same opportunities for education and employment,

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to single out a particular case or example.
Used to indicate similarity or equality between two things.
A set of circumstances that makes it possible to do something.

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for a city skyline, a school, and a factory/office building. Animate an equal sign between a girl and a boy icon.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the phrase '有了很大的提高' to describe significant improvement.

narration.subtitle_en: This is the second part of the previous sentence, stating the result of these new opportunities. It says [zh:妇女的社会地位], 'women's social status,' [zh:也], 'also,' [zh:有了很大的提高]. This phrase means 'had a great improvement.' [zh:提高] means 'improvement' or 'to improve.' The whole clause shows a direct positive consequence of equal access to education and jobs.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 妇女的社会地位也有了很大的提高。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: fùnǚ de shèhuì dìwèi yě yǒu le hěn dà de tígāo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: and women's social status improved greatly.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A person's standing in society.
Can be a noun or verb, indicating an increase in level, standard, or quality.

segments.visual_notes: Show an upward-pointing arrow next to an icon representing 'social status'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a more recent development and the difference between '企业' and '事业单位'.

narration.subtitle_en: However, the story isn't linear. This sentence introduces a setback. It starts with [zh:改革开放以来], 'since the Reform and Opening-Up,' a major period of economic change in China starting in the late 1970s. In some [zh:企业] and [zh:事业单位], the phenomenon of gender inequality, [zh:男女不平等的现象], has reappeared, [zh:又出现了]. The language notes clarify that [zh:企业] are profit-driven businesses, while [zh:事业单位] are government-funded public institutions like schools or hospitals. Both are types of [zh:单位], or 'work units'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 改革开放以来，在一些企业和事业单位，又出现了男女不平等的现象。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Gǎigé kāifàng yǐlái, zài yīxiē qǐyè hé shìyè dānwèi, yòu chūxiàn le nánnǚ bù píngděng de xiànxiàng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Since the Reform and Opening-Up, the phenomenon of gender inequality has reappeared in some enterprises and public institutions.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The period of economic reforms in China that began in 1978.
A time word used after a time point or event to mean 'since then'.
A for-profit company or business.
A government-funded organization, such as a school or hospital.
A fact or situation that is observed to exist or happen.

segments.visual_notes: Show a split screen. On one side, an icon for '企业' (factory/office). On the other, an icon for '事业单位' (school/hospital). Animate a 'reappearing' inequality sign over them.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to give examples with '比方说' and describe difficulty with '更难'.

narration.subtitle_en: The text now gives specific examples of this inequality. It begins with [zh:比方说], which means 'for example.' It's similar to '比如说'. The first example is that [zh:找工作的时候], 'when looking for a job,' women, [zh:妇女], [zh:常常], 'often,' find it [zh:更难], 'more difficult,' [zh:比男人], 'than men.' The word [zh:更] is used before an adjective to form the comparative degree, meaning 'more'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 比方说，找工作的时候，妇女常常比男人更难；
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Bǐfāng shuō, zhǎo gōngzuò de shíhou, fùnǚ chángcháng bǐ nánrén gèng nán;
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: For example, it is often more difficult for women to find jobs than men;

highlight_words.explanation_en: A phrase used to introduce an example.
The common phrase for job hunting.
An adverb used to make a comparison, placed before an adjective.

segments.visual_notes: Show a woman icon facing a closed door with a 'No Entry' sign, while a man icon walks through an open door.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the idiom '同工同酬' for 'equal pay for equal work'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's the second example of inequality. [zh:一些工厂和公司], 'some factories and companies,' [zh:还没有做到], 'have not yet achieved,' a very important concept: [zh:男女同工同酬]. This is a four-character idiom. [zh:同] means 'same,' [zh:工] means 'work,' and [zh:酬] means 'pay' or 'reward.' So, literally, it's 'same work, same pay.' The full phrase means 'equal pay for equal work for men and women'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 一些工厂和公司还没有做到男女同工同酬。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yìxiē gōngchǎng hé gōngsī hái méiyǒu zuòdào nánnǚ tóng gōng tóng chóu.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: some factories and companies have not yet achieved equal pay for equal work.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A resultative complement indicating the successful completion of an action.
An idiom meaning that people doing the same work should receive the same remuneration.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Literally 'same work, same pay.' The repetition of '同' (same) emphasizes the principle of equality.

segments.visual_notes: Show a man and a woman doing the same job. The man receives a large bag of money, while the woman receives a smaller one.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the '无论...还是...' structure and learn vocabulary for success.

narration.subtitle_en: The text now presents a counterpoint. It starts with [zh:当然], 'of course.' It says there are also some [zh:女性], 'women,' who surpass the average man. The sentence uses the structure [zh:无论...还是..., 都...], which means 'regardless of whether it's... or..., they all...'. Here, it's 'regardless of whether it's in work, [zh:在工作上], or in income, [zh:在收入上].' They [zh:都超过], 'all surpass,' the [zh:一般男人], 'average man.' [zh:超过] means 'to surpass' or 'to exceed'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 当然，也有一些女性，无论在工作上还是收入上，都超过一般男人。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dāngrán, yě yǒu yìxiē nǚxìng, wúlùn zài gōngzuò shàng háishì shōurù shàng, dōu chāoguò yìbān nánrén.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Of course, there are also some women who surpass the average man, both in their work and in their income.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A more formal or scientific term for 'woman' compared to '女人' or '女孩子'.
A conjunction used to indicate that the result remains the same under different conditions.
Money received, especially on a regular basis, for work or through investments.
To become greater than something or someone else.

segments.visual_notes: Show a woman icon on a winner's podium, standing taller than a group of 'average men' icons.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the word '少数' and the transitional word '不过'.

narration.subtitle_en: This short sentence qualifies the previous statement. It begins with [zh:不过], which means 'however' or 'but.' It's a softer transition than '但是'. The sentence says that these successful women, [zh:她们], are [zh:只是少数], 'only a minority,' [zh:在女性中], 'among women.' So, while they exist, they are not yet the norm.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 不过她们在女性中只是少数。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Búguò tāmen zài nǚxìng zhōng zhǐshì shǎoshù.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: However, they are only a minority among women.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to introduce a statement that contrasts with or seems to contradict what has been said before.
The opposite of '多数' (majority).

segments.visual_notes: Show a large group of generic female icons, with only one or two highlighted to represent the 'minority'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to family members and interactions.

narration.subtitle_en: The focus now shifts from the workplace to the home. The sentence starts with [zh:但是], 'but.' It says that [zh:在很多中国家庭中], 'in many Chinese families,' the [zh:丈夫], 'husband,' and [zh:妻子], 'wife,' [zh:都是互相照顾]. The key phrase here is [zh:互相照顾], which means 'to take care of each other.' [zh:互相] means 'mutually' or 'each other,' and [zh:照顾] means 'to look after'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 但是在很多中国家庭中，丈夫和妻子都是互相照顾，
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dànshì zài hěnduō Zhōngguó jiātíng zhōng, zhàngfu hé qīzi dōu shì hùxiāng zhàogù,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: But in many Chinese families, husbands and wives take care of each other,

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to the family unit.
A formal term for husband.
A formal term for wife.

highlight_words.translation: to take care of each other
highlight_words.explanation_en: Describes a mutual relationship of care and support.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a house. Inside, animate a husband and wife helping each other with tasks.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the conclusion that the family might be the most equal place in modern Chinese society.

narration.subtitle_en: This part draws a conclusion from the previous statement. It starts with [zh:所以], 'so' or 'therefore.' It suggests that the [zh:家庭], 'family,' [zh:可能], 'possibly' or 'may be,' the most equal place for men and women in today's Chinese society. The phrase is [zh:男女最平等的地方]. [zh:最] is the superlative, meaning '-est,' so [zh:最平等] means 'most equal.' This presents a contrast between the lingering inequality in the workplace and the growing equality within the home.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 所以，家庭可能是现在中国社会男女最平等的地方。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: suǒyǐ, jiātíng kěnéng shì xiànzài Zhōngguó shèhuì nánnǚ zuì píngděng de dìfang.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: so the family may be the most equal place for men and women in Chinese society today.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to indicate a result or consequence.
An adverb or auxiliary verb expressing possibility.
The state of being equal, especially in status, rights, or opportunities.

segments.visual_notes: Show the house icon from before, now with a glowing 'equal' sign on top, positioned as the peak of a chart comparing it to 'workplace'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the '拿...来说' structure for giving examples and some new vocabulary.

narration.subtitle_en: To illustrate the point about equality at home, the text gives a concrete example. It uses the structure [zh:拿...来说吧], which means 'Let's take... for example.' Here, it's Lin Xuemei's [zh:舅舅], her 'maternal uncle' (mother's brother). He is described as a [zh:大球迷], a 'big soccer fan.' The character [zh:大] here means 'big' in the sense of 'enthusiastic' or 'major'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 拿林雪梅的舅舅来说吧，他是个大球迷，
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Ná Lín Xuěméi de jiùjiu lái shuō ba, tā shì ge dà qiúmí,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Take Lin Xuemei's uncle for example; he is a big soccer fan,

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common structure for introducing an example.
Specifically, one's mother's brother.
A person who is an enthusiastic devotee of a ball game, especially soccer or basketball.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a character (the uncle) with a soccer ball and a fan scarf.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the '只有...才...' structure for expressing a sole condition.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence describes the one exception to the uncle's helpfulness. It uses the structure [zh:只有...才...], which means 'only when...'. The condition is [zh:在看电视里的足球赛的时候], 'when watching a soccer match on TV.' Only at this time does he not do any housework. The phrase [zh:什么家务都不做] is an emphatic way to say 'doesn't do any housework at all.' [zh:家务] means 'housework' or 'chores'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 只有在看电视里的足球赛的时候才什么家务都不做。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: zhǐyǒu zài kàn diànshì lǐ de zúqiúsài de shíhou cái shénme jiāwù dōu bù zuò.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: only when he is watching a soccer match on TV does he not do any housework.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure that introduces a single condition that must be met for a result to occur.
Composed of '足球' (soccer) and '赛' (match/competition).
Routine tasks performed in the home, such as cleaning and cooking.

segments.visual_notes: Show the uncle on a sofa, eyes glued to a TV showing a soccer game, while icons for chores (broom, dishes) have a 'pause' symbol over them.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the '一...就...' structure and the term '模范丈夫'.

narration.subtitle_en: This final sentence shows what happens after the exception. It starts with [zh:可是], 'but.' It uses the structure [zh:一...就...], meaning 'as soon as... then...'. [zh:比赛一完], 'as soon as the match is over,' [zh:他就忙着], 'he gets busy' helping his wife, [zh:舅妈], with chores like [zh:做饭], 'cooking,' and [zh:洗碗], 'washing dishes.' He then [zh:又成了], 'once again becomes,' a [zh:模范丈夫], a 'model husband.' This term is often used, sometimes humorously, to describe a husband who is very helpful around the house.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 可是比赛一完，他就忙着帮舅妈做饭、洗碗，又成了一位“模范丈夫”。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Kěshì bǐsài yī wán, tā jiù mángzhe bāng jiùmā zuòfàn, xǐwǎn, yòu chéng le yí wèi 'mófàn zhàngfu'.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: But as soon as the match is over, he gets busy helping his wife cook and wash dishes, becoming a 'model husband' again.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure indicating that the second action happens immediately after the first.
The wife of one's mother's brother.
An exemplary husband, often one who shares housework.

segments.visual_notes: Show the uncle character immediately getting up from the sofa, putting on an apron, and happily cooking and washing dishes. A golden medal with '模范丈夫' appears on his chest.

segments.segment_title: Asking Why Uncle is Washing Dishes
segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to ask 'how come' or 'why' in a casual context and learn the word for 'maternal uncle'.

narration.subtitle_en: The dialogue starts with a question to the uncle. The word for maternal uncle, your mother's brother, is [zh:舅舅]. The speaker asks, [zh:今天怎么您洗碗呀]. Let's break it down. [zh:今天] means 'today'. [zh:怎么] means 'how' or 'how come', used here to express surprise. [zh:您] is the polite form of 'you'. [zh:洗碗] means 'to wash dishes'. The final particle [zh:呀] softens the tone of the question, making it sound friendly and curious.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 舅舅，今天怎么您洗碗呀？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Jiùjiu, jīntiān zěnme nín xǐ wǎn ya?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Uncle, how come you are washing the dishes today?

highlight_words.explanation_en: The word for one's mother's brother.
A verb-object phrase; 'xǐ' means 'to wash' and 'wǎn' means 'bowl'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the characters for '舅舅' and '洗碗' as they are mentioned.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a comparative sentence using '比' and the word for 'aunt'.

narration.subtitle_en: The uncle replies with a laugh, [zh:哈哈]. He says, [zh:因为我洗碗比你舅妈洗得干净啊]. [zh:因为] means 'because'. He is making a comparison using the structure 'A 比 B + Adj.'. Here, he says 'I wash dishes cleaner than your aunt does'. [zh:舅妈] is the word for your maternal uncle's wife. The key adjective is [zh:干净], meaning 'clean'. The particle [zh:啊] at the end adds a casual, proud tone to his statement.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哈哈，因为我洗碗比你舅妈洗得干净啊。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hāhā, yīnwèi wǒ xǐ wǎn bǐ nǐ jiùmā xǐ de gānjìng a.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Haha, because I wash dishes cleaner than your aunt does.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to introduce a reason or cause.
The wife of one's 'jiùjiu'.
An adjective describing something as clean or tidy.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the 'A 比 B ... 干净' structure on screen.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to offer help with '我来...吧' and see the emphatic '是...的' structure in action.

narration.subtitle_en: The niece then offers to help. She says, [zh:我来洗吧]. This is a common and polite way to say 'Let me do it'. She then adds, [zh:我洗碗也是洗得很干净的]. Notice the [zh:是...的] structure here. It adds a persuasive and confident tone, as if to say, 'I *also* do a very good job at washing dishes.' It's used to reassure or emphasize a point, which we will cover in more detail later.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我来洗吧！我洗碗也是洗得很干净的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ lái xǐ ba! Wǒ xǐ wǎn yě shì xǐ de hěn gānjìng de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Let me wash them! I also wash dishes very cleanly.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common phrase used to volunteer to do something.
A sentence structure used to add a persuasive or reassuring tone.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the phrase '我来...吧' and highlight the '是...的' structure in the second sentence.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to refuse an offer politely and learn the complement '过来' indicating recovery.

narration.subtitle_en: The aunt gently refuses. She starts with [zh:别、别、别], repeating 'don't' to be emphatic but kind. She explains that the niece and her companion just got back from a trip to Yunnan, [zh:从云南旅游回来], and have been busy with job searching, [zh:找工作], and interviews, [zh:面试]. The key phrase here is [zh:还没休息过来呢]. [zh:休息] is 'to rest', and the complement [zh:过来] implies recovering or returning to a normal, rested state. So, it means 'you haven't rested up yet'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 别、别、别，你们从云南旅游回来，就忙着找工作、面试，还没休息过来呢。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Bié, bié, bié, nǐmen cóng Yúnnán lǚyóu huílái, jiù mángzhe zhǎo gōngzuò, miànshì, hái méi xiūxi guolai ne.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: No, no, no, you just got back from your trip to Yunnan and are already busy looking for jobs and interviewing, you haven't rested yet.
on_screen_text.notes: This is the first part of a longer sentence.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A formal meeting for a job application.

highlight_words.translation: to be rested up, to recover from fatigue
highlight_words.explanation_en: The complement 'guolai' indicates a return to a normal, rested state.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight '休息过来' and its meaning of 'recovery'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the use of '再说' and a rhetorical question for making a point.

narration.subtitle_en: The aunt continues with her second reason, starting with [zh:再说], which means 'besides' or 'furthermore'. She explains that the uncle, [zh:你舅舅], watched a ball game, [zh:看球], until one in the morning, [zh:夜里一点]. Then she asks a rhetorical question: [zh:今天还不得好好表现表现]. This means, 'shouldn't he put on a good show today?' implying he needs to make up for staying up late by doing chores. Reduplicating the verb [zh:表现] makes it sound more casual.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 再说，你舅舅呀，昨天晚上看球看到夜里一点，今天还不得好好表现表现？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zàishuō, nǐ jiùjiu ya, zuótiān wǎnshang kàn qiú kàn dào yèlǐ yīdiǎn, jīntiān hái bù děi hǎohǎo biǎoxiàn biǎoxiàn?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Besides, your uncle watched the game until one in the morning last night, shouldn't he put on a good show today?
on_screen_text.notes: This is the second part of a longer sentence.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to introduce an additional reason.
Here it means to behave well or put on a good performance, often to impress someone.

segments.visual_notes: Show a clock icon for '夜里一点' and highlight the rhetorical question.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask about a specific event in the past.

narration.subtitle_en: Curious, the niece asks her uncle about the game. She says, [zh:舅舅，昨天晚上是什么比赛啊]. This is a straightforward question. [zh:昨天晚上] means 'last night'. The core of the question is [zh:是什么比赛], 'what match was it?'. The word [zh:比赛] means 'match' or 'competition'. Again, the particle [zh:啊] at the end makes the question sound casual and friendly.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 舅舅，昨天晚上是什么比赛啊？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Jiùjiu, zuótiān wǎnshang shì shénme bǐsài a?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Uncle, what game was on last night?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A contest or game where teams or individuals compete against each other.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a trophy or a scoreboard to represent '比赛'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for sports teams and how to state 'Team A vs. Team B'.

narration.subtitle_en: The uncle gives a specific answer. He says, [zh:男子足球赛，北京队对上海队]. Let's break this down. [zh:男子] means 'men's' or 'male'. [zh:足球] is 'soccer', and [zh:赛] is 'match'. So, [zh:男子足球赛] is a 'men's soccer match'. He then names the teams using the pattern 'A 对 B', which means 'A versus B'. So, it was the Beijing team, [zh:北京队], against the Shanghai team, [zh:上海队].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 男子足球赛，北京队对上海队。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nánzǐ zúqiú sài, Běijīng duì duì Shànghǎi duì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: A men's soccer match, the Beijing team against the Shanghai team.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to the male gender.
Literally 'foot ball'.
A group of people forming one side in a competition.
Used in sports to indicate opposition between two teams.

segments.visual_notes: Display the logos of Beijing and Shanghai with a 'VS' symbol between them.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express a strong negative opinion and use phrases like '得不得了'.

narration.subtitle_en: The niece expresses her strong opinion. She starts with a rhetorical question, [zh:那有什么好看的], meaning 'What's so good about that?'. She then says she can't stand the men's soccer players, [zh:我最讨厌那些男足队员了]. [zh:讨厌] means 'to dislike' or 'can't stand'. She says they are usually, [zh:平常], incredibly arrogant, [zh:骄傲得不得了]. The phrase [zh:得不得了] means 'extremely'. But when it's time to compete, they always, [zh:老], lose, [zh:输球].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 那有什么好看的？我最讨厌那些男足队员了，平常骄傲得不得了，可是比赛的时候老输球。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nà yǒu shénme hǎokàn de? Wǒ zuì tǎoyàn nàxiē nánzú duìyuán le, píngcháng jiāo'ào de bùdéliǎo, kěshì bǐsài de shíhou lǎo shū qiú.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What's so good about that? I can't stand those men's soccer players. They are usually incredibly arrogant, but they always lose games.
on_screen_text.notes: This is the first part of a longer sentence.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Expresses a strong feeling of aversion.
Can mean proud in a positive sense, but here it means arrogant.
The opposite of 'yíng qiú' (to win a ball game).

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the phrase '得不得了' to emphasize 'extremely'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to state a preference and make a comparison using '比...棒多了'.

narration.subtitle_en: Continuing her point, she says, [zh:所以，我只看中国女足]. [zh:所以] means 'so', and [zh:只] means 'only'. She only watches the Chinese women's soccer team, [zh:中国女足]. She finishes with a comparison: [zh:她们比男足棒多了]. This means 'They are much better than the men's team'. [zh:棒] is a colloquial word for 'great' or 'awesome', and adding [zh:多了] after the adjective in a comparison emphasizes that the difference is large.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 所以，我只看中国女足，她们比男足棒多了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Suǒyǐ, wǒ zhǐ kàn Zhōngguó nǚzú, tāmen bǐ nánzú bàng duō le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: So, I only watch the Chinese women's team; they are much better than the men's team.
on_screen_text.notes: This is the second part of a longer sentence.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Short for '女子足球队' (women's soccer team).
A colloquial adjective to praise something or someone.

segments.visual_notes: Show a thumbs-up icon next to the word '棒'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to performance and fairness, and to be introduced to fractions.

narration.subtitle_en: The uncle agrees, saying [zh:不错], which means 'That's right' or 'Not bad'. He confirms the women's team has better results, [zh:成绩好]. But, [zh:可是], he brings up a new point. He says, 'I heard the female players earn only one-tenth of what the male players earn.' The key words are [zh:挣钱] (to earn money) and the fraction [zh:十分之一] (one-tenth). He concludes that this is [zh:真不公平], 'really unfair'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 不错，女足是比男足成绩好，可是我听说女足队员挣的钱只有男足的十分之一，真不公平。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Búcuò, nǚzú shì bǐ nánzú chéngjì hǎo, kěshì wǒ tīngshuō nǚzú duìyuán zhèng de qián zhǐyǒu nánzú de shí fēn zhī yī, zhēn bù gōngpíng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: That's right, the women's team does have better results than the men's team, but I heard that the female players earn only one-tenth of what the male players earn. That's really unfair.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to performance, scores, or achievements.
A fraction, literally 'of ten parts, one'.
The opposite of 'gōngpíng' (fair).

segments.visual_notes: Visually represent '十分之一' as a pie chart with one of ten slices highlighted.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the structure for forming fractions in Chinese, using '十分之一' as an example.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's look at how fractions are formed in Chinese. The phrase is [zh:十分之一]. The structure is 'Denominator + [zh:分之] + Numerator'. So, [zh:十] is the denominator, 10. [zh:一] is the numerator, 1. And [zh:分之] literally means 'parts of'. So, [zh:十分之一] is 'of ten parts, one', or one-tenth. Using this pattern, three-fourths would be 'four parts of, three', which is [zh:四分之三].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 十分之一
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: shí fēn zhī yī
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: one-tenth (1/10)

highlight_words.explanation_en: A fraction meaning 1/10. The structure is Denominator + 分之 + Numerator.

segments.visual_notes: Show the formula 'Denominator + 分之 + Numerator' on screen with examples like 1/10 and 3/4.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to economics and professional sports.

narration.subtitle_en: The aunt offers a different perspective. She says, [zh:别忘了], 'Don't forget'. She explains that their income, [zh:收入], is determined by the market, [zh:是由市场来决定的]. She then gives another example, comparing the salaries, [zh:薪水], of professional female basketball players in the U.S. with male players. The phrase [zh:跟...比] means 'compared with'. She concludes that their salaries are also 'much less', [zh:也少多了].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 别忘了，他们的收入是由市场来决定的。美国职业女篮队员的薪水跟职业男篮比，也少多了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Bié wàng le, tāmen de shōurù shì yóu shìchǎng lái juédìng de. Měiguó zhíyè nǚlán duìyuán de xīnshuǐ gēn zhíyè nánlán bǐ, yě shǎo duō le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Don't forget, their income is determined by the market. The salaries of professional female basketball players in the U.S. are also much lower than those of male players.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Money received on a regular basis, for work or through investments.
An economic system where things are bought and sold.
To be the cause or reason for something.
A fixed regular payment from an employer.

segments.visual_notes: Use icons for 'income' (money bag) and 'market' (graph).

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a sentence delivering surprising news and learn vocabulary for 'online' and 'win'.

narration.subtitle_en: The uncle interrupts with some surprising news to change the subject. He starts with [zh:哎], an interjection, and says, [zh:别老说中国男足输球了], 'Stop always talking about the Chinese men's team losing.' He says he just saw news, [zh:消息], online, [zh:网上]. And the news is... the men's team won, [zh:赢了], against the world champion team, [zh:世界冠军队], yesterday! This unexpected twist adds a humorous end to the conversation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哎，别老说中国男足输球了。我刚看到网上的消息说，中国男足昨天赢了世界冠军队。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Āi, bié lǎo shuō Zhōngguó nánzú shū qiú le. Wǒ gāng kàndào wǎngshàng de xiāoxi shuō, Zhōngguó nánzú zuótiān yíng le shìjiè guànjūn duì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Hey, don't always talk about the Chinese men's team losing. I just saw news online saying the Chinese men's team beat the world champion team yesterday.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'on the net'.
Information about recent events.
The opposite of 'shū' (to lose).
'shìjiè' means 'world' and 'guànjūn' means 'champion'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a 'Breaking News' graphic with a trophy icon next to '赢了'.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to express disbelief and speculate that something is a rumor from the internet.

narration.subtitle_en: The first speaker expresses disbelief. They say [zh:我不相信], which means 'I don't believe it.' Then, they guess about the source of the information with the phrase [zh:是网上乱说的吧]. Let's break this down. [zh:网上] means 'on the internet.' [zh:乱说] means 'to speak nonsense' or 'to spread rumors.' The final particle [zh:吧] turns the statement into a speculative question, like adding '...right?' in English. So, the full sentence implies, 'This must be some nonsense from the internet, right?'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我不相信，是网上乱说的吧？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ bù xiāngxìn, shì wǎngshàng luànshuō de ba?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I don't believe it. It must be some nonsense from the internet, right?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb meaning to believe or to trust in something or someone.
Literally 'on the net,' this word is used to describe things happening online.
A verb that means to speak carelessly, talk nonsense, or spread rumors. '乱' means chaotic or random, and '说' means to speak.

segments.visual_notes: An icon of a person shrugging next to a computer screen with question marks.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to report information from a source and describe the status of a sports game.

narration.subtitle_en: The second speaker confirms the news, but with a slight hedge. They start with [zh:好像是真的], meaning 'It seems to be true.' Then, they cite their source: [zh:网上说], 'The internet says...' This is a very common way to report something you've read online. The sentence continues, [zh:这次中国男足踢得不错], 'This time the Chinese men's soccer team played quite well.' Finally, they describe the game's state: [zh:比分一直是0比0], 'the score was consistently 0 to 0.' [zh:比分] is the word for 'score' in a game.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 好像是真的。网上说，这次中国男足踢得不错，比分一直是0比0。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hǎoxiàng shì zhēn de. Wǎngshàng shuō, zhè cì Zhōngguó nánzú tī de búcuò, bǐfēn yìzhí shì 0 bǐ 0.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: It seems to be true. The internet says the Chinese men's team played well this time, the score was 0-0 for a long time.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to express that something appears to be a certain way, but you're not 100% sure.
A compound word from '男' (nán, male) and '足球' (zúqiú, soccer).
The numerical score of a competition. '比' means to compare and '分' means points.

segments.visual_notes: An animation showing a soccer scoreboard with the score '0-0'.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to describe the climax and result of a game, using words like '才' and '以'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's the exciting conclusion of the story. It begins with [zh:最后一分钟], 'in the last minute.' The word [zh:才] in [zh:才踢进一球] is important; it emphasizes that the goal was scored very late, 'only then did they score a goal.' The phrase [zh:踢进一球] means 'to kick in one goal.' The final result is given with [zh:以1比0赢了比赛], 'won the game with a score of 1 to 0.' The word [zh:以] means 'with' or 'by means of,' and [zh:赢了比赛] means 'won the match.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 最后一分钟，中国男足才踢进一球，以1比0赢了比赛。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zuìhòu yì fēnzhōng, Zhōngguó nánzú cái tī jìn yì qiú, yǐ 1 bǐ 0 yíng le bǐsài.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: In the last minute, the men's team scored a goal and won the game 1-0.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb used to indicate that an action happened later than expected.
A resultative complement. '踢' means to kick, and '进' means to enter. Together, they mean to kick something in.
The verb for winning a game, competition, or bet.

segments.visual_notes: Animation of a soccer ball flying into a net, and the scoreboard changing to '1-0'.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn common phrases to express surprise and excitement, like '没想到' and '太棒了'.

narration.subtitle_en: The first speaker reacts with great surprise and excitement. The phrase [zh:没想到] is a fantastic one to learn; it means 'I didn't expect that' or 'who would have thought.' Repeating it with [zh:真的] in front, as in [zh:真的没想到], adds strong emphasis: 'I really didn't expect that!' This is followed by the exclamation [zh:太棒了], which means 'That's great!' or 'Awesome!' It's a common way to express delight.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 没想到，真的没想到，太棒了！
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Méi xiǎngdào, zhēn de méi xiǎngdào, tài bàng le!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I didn't expect that, really didn't expect that, that's great!

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'didn't think of,' this phrase is used to express surprise about something unforeseen.
An adverb used to add emphasis, similar to 'really' or 'truly' in English.
A colloquial adjective for 'excellent' or 'wonderful'.

segments.visual_notes: An animated character with a surprised expression and stars popping around their head.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to use the '是...还是...' structure to ask a choice question.

narration.subtitle_en: Now, the speaker asks a follow-up question based on their assumption. The question is, [zh:那这个世界冠军队是德国队还是西班牙队]. Let's break it down. [zh:世界冠军队] means 'world champion team.' The core of the question uses the '[zh:是] A [zh:还是] B' structure for asking 'is it A or B?'. Here, the options are [zh:德国队], the German team, and [zh:西班牙队], the Spanish team. This is the standard way to form a choice question in Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 那这个世界冠军队是德国队还是西班牙队？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nà zhège shìjiè guànjūn duì shì Déguó duì háishì Xībānyá duì?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: So was that world champion team the German team or the Spanish team?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A combination of '世界' (shìjiè, world) and '冠军' (guànjūn, champion).
A noun for a team or group.
Used in questions to offer a choice between two or more options.

segments.visual_notes: Visuals of the German and Spanish flags with a large 'OR' (还是) between them.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand the punchline of the joke and how sports teams are named.

narration.subtitle_en: This short sentence is the punchline of the entire joke. The answer is not a famous soccer team, but the [zh:中国乒乓球女队], 'The Chinese women's table tennis team.' This reveals the clever twist. The story was never about the men's soccer team, or [zh:男足], but about the women's table tennis team, the [zh:女队]. This sudden switch from soccer to table tennis, and from the men's team to the women's team, is what makes the situation humorous.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 中国乒乓球女队。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhōngguó pīngpāngqiú nǚduì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The Chinese women's table tennis team.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An onomatopoeic word, mimicking the sound of the ball hitting the paddle and table.
A compound word from '女' (nǚ, female) and '队' (duì, team).

segments.visual_notes: An image of a soccer ball morphing into a ping-pong ball.

segments.teaching_goal: Recognize and use the interjection '啊' to show confusion or surprise.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a simple but very expressive response. The character says [zh:啊] with a rising tone, which is written as 'á' in pinyin. It's the perfect equivalent of saying 'Huh?' or 'What?' in English when you are confused or completely surprised by what you just heard. The speaker is clearly trying to process the unexpected information about the table tennis team.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 啊？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Á?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Huh?

highlight_words.explanation_en: An interjection used to express doubt, surprise, or to ask for repetition. The tone can change its meaning.

segments.visual_notes: A large question mark appears over the character's head.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to express a realization, using the phrase '开玩笑' (to make a joke).

narration.subtitle_en: Finally, the penny drops! The speaker realizes what's going on. They exclaim, [zh:那一定是什么人在开男足的玩笑]. The word [zh:那] at the beginning means 'In that case...' or 'Then...'. [zh:一定] means 'definitely' or 'must be.' The key phrase here is [zh:开玩笑], 'to make a joke.' Notice the structure: [zh:开], then the object of the joke [zh:男足的], and then [zh:玩笑]. This '开...的玩笑' pattern is how you say you're making a joke about someone or something.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 那一定是什么人在开男足的玩笑！
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nà yídìng shì shénme rén zài kāi nánzú de wánxiào!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Then someone must be making a joke about the men's soccer team!

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb expressing certainty or conviction.

highlight_words.translation: to joke; to make fun of
highlight_words.explanation_en: A common verb-object phrase meaning to tell a joke or to be kidding. The structure '开 + [someone's] + 玩笑' means to make fun of someone.

segments.visual_notes: An animation of a lightbulb turning on above the speaker's head.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach students how to use the 是…的 structure to add emphasis, reassurance, or a persuasive tone to a statement.

narration.subtitle_en: Now, let's focus on a useful grammar pattern for adding emphasis. This is the second usage of the [zh:是...的] construction. While we've seen it used for past events, here it serves to persuade, reassure, or simply make a statement more emphatic. In the dialogue, Lin Xuemei says [zh:我洗碗也是洗得很干净的], which means 'I also do a good job at washing dishes.' The [zh:是...的] frames the phrase 'wash very cleanly,' making her statement more convincing and reassuring. Without it, the sentence is more neutral. This structure often works with adjectives, like [zh:是很不错的] meaning 'is quite good,' or with verbs that are paired with modal verbs like [zh:会]. For example, [zh:他是会丢三拉四的] means 'He *does* get scatterbrained.' Remember, this pattern adds a layer of certainty or emphasis to your statement.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我洗碗也是洗得很干净的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ xǐwǎn yě shì xǐ de hěn gānjìng de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I also wash dishes very cleanly. (emphatic)
on_screen_text.notes: The 是...的 structure adds a persuasive or reassuring tone.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Used to make an emphatic statement with an adjective. Example: 住在校内是很安全的。(It is very safe to live on campus.)
Used with the modal verb 会 to emphasize a tendency or possibility. Example: 他是会丢三拉四的。(He does get scatterbrained.)
Used with a potential complement to emphasize that something is achievable. Example: 你的电脑是找得到的。(Your computer will be found.)

segments.visual_notes: Animate the words '是' and '的' to show how they frame the central phrase. Use a 'thumbs up' or 'sparkle' icon to represent the emphatic/reassuring tone.

segments.teaching_goal: To teach the use of '过来' as a resultative complement indicating a return to a normal or active state.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, let's look at the complement [zh:过来]. When attached to a verb, [zh:过来] can indicate a return to a normal or active state. The aunt uses it when she says [zh:还没休息过来呢], meaning 'you haven't rested up or recovered yet.' She's implying that Lin Xuemei is still tired from her trip and hasn't returned to her normal, rested state. Another powerful example is [zh:救过来], which means to save someone and bring them back from the brink of death. You can also use it for mental states, like in [zh:明白过来], which means to finally understand or have something click. The opposite of [zh:过来] in this usage is [zh:过去], as in [zh:晕过去了], meaning 'to faint' or 'to pass out'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 还没休息过来呢。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: hái méi xiūxi guòlái ne.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: ...(you) haven't rested up yet.
on_screen_text.notes: Verb + 过来 indicates a return to a normal state.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Indicates a return to a normal or active state after an action.

grammar_points.pattern: 他病得很厉害，医生费了好大力气才把他救过来。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Tā bìng de hěn lìhai, yīshēng fèi le hǎo dà lìqi cái bǎ tā jiù guolai.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Example: 'He was seriously ill. The doctor barely managed to save him.' Here, '救过来' means to save and bring back to a state of being alive.
Example: '...only then did it become clear to him.' Here, '明白过来' means to transition from a state of confusion to understanding.

segments.visual_notes: Use an arrow icon that curves back to a starting point to visualize the 'return to normal' concept. For '休息过来', show a battery icon charging up. For '明白过来', show a lightbulb turning on.

segments.teaching_goal: To summarize the key grammar points and vocabulary from the lesson.

narration.subtitle_en: Great job. Let's quickly recap the main points of this lesson. We learned how to use the emphatic [zh:是...的] structure to make our statements more persuasive. We also covered the resultative complement [zh:过来], which signifies a return to a normal state. Finally, we discussed important vocabulary related to gender equality, such as [zh:重男轻女] and [zh:同工同酬].

segments.teaching_goal: Comprendre le contexte historique de l'inégalité des sexes en Chine et apprendre l'expression clé [zh:重男轻女].

narration_track.subtitle_en: Le texte commence par poser le contexte historique. Il dit « [zh:在历史上] », ce qui signifie « dans l'histoire » ou « historiquement ». Ensuite, il indique que la Chine était une société qui privilégiait les hommes par rapport aux femmes. L'expression clé ici est « [zh:重男轻女] ». Décomposons-la : « [zh:重] » signifie « valoriser » ou « considérer comme important ». « [zh:男] » est « homme ». « [zh:轻] » signifie « dévaloriser » ou « considérer comme sans importance ». Et « [zh:女] » est « femme ». Ensemble, « [zh:重男轻女] » décrit une société ou une mentalité qui valorise davantage les hommes que les femmes.

content.focus_text: 在历史上，中国是一个重男轻女的社会，
content.focus_pinyin: Zài lìshǐ shàng, Zhōngguó shì yí ge zhòng nán qīng nǚ de shèhuì,
content.focus_gloss_en: Historiquement, la Chine était une société qui privilégiait les hommes par rapport aux femmes,

highlight_words.explanation_en: Désigne les événements passés ou leur étude.

highlight_words.translation: privilégier les hommes par rapport aux femmes
highlight_words.explanation_en: Une expression idiomatique décrivant la préférence traditionnelle pour les hommes par rapport aux femmes.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Literally 'heavy male, light female,' vividly capturing the idea of valuing one gender over the other.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Désigne une communauté de personnes vivant dans un pays ou une région spécifique, partageant des coutumes, des lois et des organisations.
Désigne les événements passés ou leur étude.

highlight_words.translation: privilégier les hommes par rapport aux femmes
highlight_words.explanation_en: Une expression idiomatique décrivant la préférence traditionnelle pour les hommes par rapport aux femmes.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Literally 'heavy male, light female,' vividly capturing the idea of valuing one gender over the other.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Désigne une communauté de personnes vivant dans un pays ou une région spécifique, partageant des coutumes, des lois et des organisations.

segments.segment_title: Comparaison des statuts : Femmes contre hommes
segments.teaching_goal: Apprendre à exprimer la comparaison avec '比...得多' et à comprendre le concept de '地位'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cette partie de la phrase décrit les conséquences de cette vision sociétale. Elle parle du statut des « [zh:妇女] », un terme formel pour « femmes ». Leur « [zh:家庭地位] », leur statut familial, et leur « [zh:社会地位] », leur statut social, étaient tous deux bien inférieurs à ceux des hommes. Le mot « [zh:地位] » signifie « statut » ou « position ». La structure « [zh:比男人低得多] » est une comparaison clé. « [zh:比] » signifie « comparé à ». « [zh:低] » signifie « bas ». Et « [zh:得多] » ajoute de l'emphase, signifiant « beaucoup plus ». Donc, « beaucoup plus bas que les hommes ».

content.focus_text: 妇女的家庭地位和社会地位都比男人低得多。
content.focus_pinyin: fùnǚ de jiātíng dìwèi hé shèhuì dìwèi dōu bǐ nánrén dī de duō.
content.focus_gloss_en: le statut des femmes dans la famille et la société était bien inférieur à celui des hommes.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Un terme formel pour désigner les femmes, souvent employé dans des contextes sociaux ou politiques.
Désigne la position sociale ou professionnelle d'une personne.
Utilisé ici pour décrire un statut, mais peut aussi signifier une faible hauteur ou un niveau bas.
Un terme formel pour désigner les femmes, souvent employé dans des contextes sociaux ou politiques.
Désigne la position sociale ou professionnelle d'une personne.
Utilisé ici pour décrire un statut, mais peut aussi signifier une faible hauteur ou un niveau bas.

segments.visual_notes: Utilisez une icône de balance, avec le côté 'femme' nettement plus bas que le côté 'homme'. Mettez en évidence '家庭地位' et '社会地位' lorsqu'ils sont mentionnés.

segments.teaching_goal: Comprendre la phrase décrivant un changement de situation et apprendre l'adverbe '逐渐'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: La phrase suivante marque un tournant. Elle commence par « [zh:一九五○年以后] », ce qui signifie « après 1950 ». Le sujet est « [zh:情况] », qui signifie « la situation » ou « les circonstances ». L'expression verbale clé est « [zh:发生了变化] », qui signifie « a subi un changement ». Remarquez l'adverbe « [zh:逐渐] », qui signifie « progressivement ». Il indique que le changement ne s'est pas produit du jour au lendemain. Comme le mentionnent les notes linguistiques, « [zh:逐渐] » est similaire à « [zh:渐渐] », mais il est légèrement plus formel.

content.focus_text: 一九五○年以后，情况逐渐发生了变化。
content.focus_pinyin: Yījiǔwǔlíng nián yǐhòu, qíngkuàng zhújiàn fāshēng le biànhuà.
content.focus_gloss_en: Après 1950, la situation a progressivement commencé à changer.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Désigne l'état des choses à un moment donné.
Un adverbe qui indique un processus lent et progressif. Il est plus formel que '[zh:jiànjiàn]'.
Peut être un nom ou un verbe, signifiant 'changement' ou 'changer'.
Désigne l'état des choses à un moment donné.
Un adverbe qui indique un processus lent et progressif. Il est plus formel que '[zh:jiànjiàn]'.
Peut être un nom ou un verbe, signifiant 'changement' ou 'changer'.

segments.teaching_goal: Pour comprendre les améliorations spécifiques pour les femmes dans les villes, en se concentrant sur l'éducation et les opportunités d'emploi.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cette phrase précise où et comment le changement s'est produit. Elle commence par [zh:特别是在城市里], qui signifie « surtout dans les villes ». Elle indique ensuite que les filles, [zh:女孩子], et les garçons, [zh:男孩子], avaient les mêmes opportunités. La structure [zh:一样有...的机会] veut dire « avoir également l'opportunité de... ». Quelles étaient ces opportunités ? Premièrement, [zh:受教育], c'est-à-dire « recevoir une éducation ». Deuxièmement, [zh:参加工作], soit « participer à la vie active » ou « obtenir un emploi ».

content.focus_text: 特别是在城市里，女孩子和男孩子一样有受教育和参加工作的机会，
content.focus_pinyin: Tèbié shì zài chéngshì lǐ, nǚháizi hé nánháizi yíyàng yǒu shòu jiàoyù hé cānjiā gōngzuò de jīhuì,
content.focus_gloss_en: Surtout dans les villes, les filles et les garçons avaient les mêmes opportunités en matière d'éducation et d'emploi,

highlight_words.explanation_en: Utilisé pour mettre en évidence un cas ou un exemple particulier.
Utilisé pour indiquer la similarité ou l'égalité entre deux choses.
Un ensemble de circonstances qui rend possible de faire quelque chose.

Utilisé pour indiquer la similarité ou l'égalité entre deux choses.

segments.visual_notes: Afficher des icônes pour une ligne d'horizon urbaine, une école et un bâtiment d'usine ou de bureaux. Animer un signe égal entre une icône de fille et une icône de garçon.

segments.teaching_goal: Apprendre l'expression [zh:有了很大的提高] pour décrire une amélioration significative.

narration_track.subtitle_en: C'est la deuxième partie de la phrase précédente. Elle énonce le résultat de ces nouvelles opportunités. Elle dit que [zh:妇女的社会地位], le « statut social des femmes », [zh:也], « aussi », [zh:有了很大的提高]. Cette expression signifie « a connu une grande amélioration ». [zh:提高] veut dire « amélioration » ou « s'améliorer ». L'ensemble de la proposition montre une conséquence positive directe de l'égalité d'accès à l'éducation et à l'emploi.

content.focus_text: 妇女的社会地位也有了很大的提高。
content.focus_pinyin: fùnǚ de shèhuì dìwèi yě yǒu le hěn dà de tígāo.
content.focus_gloss_en: et le statut social des femmes s'est grandement amélioré.

highlight_words.explanation_en: La position d'une personne dans la société.
Peut être un nom ou un verbe, indiquant une augmentation du niveau, du standard ou de la qualité.

segments.visual_notes: Afficher une flèche pointant vers le haut à côté d'une icône représentant le 'statut social'.

segments.teaching_goal: Comprendre un développement plus récent et la différence entre [zh:企业] et [zh:事业单位].

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cependant, l'histoire n'est pas linéaire. Cette phrase introduit un revers. Elle commence par [zh:改革开放以来], qui signifie « depuis la Réforme et l'Ouverture ». C'est une période majeure de changement économique en Chine, débutant à la fin des années 1970. Dans certaines [zh:企业] et [zh:事业单位], le phénomène d'inégalité des sexes, [zh:男女不平等的现象], est réapparu. Le texte utilise l'expression [zh:又出现了]. Les notes linguistiques précisent que les [zh:企业] sont des entreprises à but lucratif. Les [zh:事业单位] sont des institutions publiques financées par le gouvernement, comme les écoles ou les hôpitaux. Les deux sont des types de [zh:单位], ou « unités de travail ».

content.focus_text: 改革开放以来，在一些企业和事业单位，又出现了男女不平等的现象。
content.focus_pinyin: Gǎigé kāifàng yǐlái, zài yīxiē qǐyè hé shìyè dānwèi, yòu chūxiàn le nánnǚ bù píngděng de xiànxiàng.
content.focus_gloss_en: Depuis la Réforme et l'Ouverture, le phénomène d'inégalité de genre est réapparu dans certaines entreprises et institutions publiques.

segments.segment_title: Exemple : Difficulté à trouver un emploi
segments.teaching_goal: Apprendre à donner des exemples avec '比方说' et à décrire une difficulté avec '更难'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Le texte donne maintenant des exemples concrets de cette inégalité. Cela commence par [zh:比方说], qui signifie « par exemple ». C'est similaire à « 比如说 ». Le premier exemple est que [zh:找工作的时候], « quand elles cherchent un emploi », les femmes, [zh:妇女], [zh:常常], « souvent », trouvent cela [zh:更难], « plus difficile », [zh:比男人], « que les hommes ». Le mot [zh:更] est utilisé devant un adjectif pour former le comparatif, signifiant « plus ».

content.focus_text: 比方说，找工作的时候，妇女常常比男人更难；
content.focus_pinyin: Bǐfāng shuō, zhǎo gōngzuò de shíhou, fùnǚ chángcháng bǐ nánrén gèng nán;
content.focus_gloss_en: Par exemple, il est souvent plus difficile pour les femmes de trouver un emploi que pour les hommes ;

highlight_words.explanation_en: Une expression utilisée pour introduire un exemple.
L'expression courante pour la recherche d'emploi.
Un adverbe utilisé pour faire une comparaison, placé avant un adjectif.

segments.visual_notes: Montrer une icône de femme face à une porte fermée avec un panneau 'Interdit d'entrer', tandis qu'une icône d'homme passe par une porte ouverte.

segments.teaching_goal: Apprendre l'expression idiomatique '同工同酬' qui signifie 'à travail égal, salaire égal'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Voici le deuxième exemple d'inégalité. [zh:一些工厂和公司], « certaines usines et entreprises », [zh:还没有做到], « n'ont pas encore mis en œuvre », un concept très important : [zh:男女同工同酬]. C'est une expression de quatre caractères. [zh:同] signifie « même », [zh:工] signifie « travail », et [zh:酬] signifie « salaire » ou « récompense ». Donc, littéralement, c'est « même travail, même salaire ». L'expression complète signifie « à travail égal, salaire égal pour les hommes et les femmes ».

content.focus_text: 一些工厂和公司还没有做到男女同工同酬。
content.focus_pinyin: yìxiē gōngchǎng hé gōngsī hái méiyǒu zuòdào nánnǚ tóng gōng tóng chóu.
content.focus_gloss_en: certaines usines et entreprises n'ont pas encore mis en place le principe 'à travail égal, salaire égal'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Un complément résultatif qui indique l'achèvement réussi d'une action.
Une expression idiomatique qui signifie que les personnes effectuant le même travail devraient recevoir la même rémunération.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Literally 'same work, same pay.' The repetition of '同' (same) emphasizes the principle of equality.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Un complément résultatif qui indique l'achèvement réussi d'une action.

segments.visual_notes: Montrer un homme et une femme effectuant le même travail. L'homme reçoit un grand sac d'argent, tandis que la femme en reçoit un plus petit.

segments.teaching_goal: Comprendre la structure '无论...还是...' et apprendre le vocabulaire lié au succès.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Le texte présente maintenant un contre-argument. Cela commence par [zh:当然], qui signifie « bien sûr ». Il est dit qu'il y a aussi des [zh:女性], « femmes », qui surpassent l'homme moyen. La phrase utilise la structure [zh:无论...还是..., 都...], qui signifie « que ce soit... ou..., elles... ». Ici, c'est « que ce soit dans le travail, [zh:在工作上], ou dans les revenus, [zh:在收入上] ». Elles [zh:都超过], « surpassent toutes », l'[zh:一般男人], « homme moyen ». [zh:超过] signifie « surpasser » ou « dépasser ».

content.focus_text: 当然，也有一些女性，无论在工作上还是收入上，都超过一般男人。
content.focus_pinyin: Dāngrán, yě yǒu yìxiē nǚxìng, wúlùn zài gōngzuò shàng háishì shōurù shàng, dōu chāoguò yìbān nánrén.
content.focus_gloss_en: Bien sûr, il y a aussi des femmes qui dépassent l'homme moyen, tant dans leur travail que dans leurs revenus.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Un terme plus formel ou scientifique pour désigner une femme, par rapport à '女人' ou '女孩子'.
Une conjonction utilisée pour indiquer que le résultat reste le même quelles que soient les conditions.
L'argent reçu, en particulier de manière régulière, pour un travail ou via des investissements.

segments.teaching_goal: Comprendre le mot '少数' et le mot de transition '不过'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cette courte phrase nuance l'affirmation précédente. Elle commence par [zh:不过], qui signifie « cependant » ou « mais ». C'est une transition plus douce que « 但是 ». La phrase indique que ces femmes qui réussissent, [zh:她们], ne sont [zh:只是少数], « qu'une minorité », [zh:在女性中], « parmi les femmes ». Donc, même si elles existent, elles ne sont pas encore la norme.

content.focus_text: 不过她们在女性中只是少数。
content.focus_pinyin: Búguò tāmen zài nǚxìng zhōng zhǐshì shǎoshù.
content.focus_gloss_en: Cependant, elles ne représentent qu'une minorité parmi les femmes.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Une conjonction utilisée pour introduire une affirmation qui contraste avec ce qui a été dit précédemment ou semble le contredire.
L'opposé de '多数' (majorité).
